The Problem

You Know What to Do.
You're Just Not Doing It.

External

The Inconsistency Cycle

You post when you remember, go quiet for weeks, then scramble back. Your strategy is "when I have time" — which means almost never.

Internal

The Quiet Guilt

Every silent week feels like falling behind. You watch other founders building audiences and wonder why you can't stay consistent. It's not a content problem. It's an accountability problem.

Philosophical

Your Work Deserves to Be Seen

You built your business to make an impact. If nobody sees your work, none of it matters. Showing up consistently isn't vanity — it's the foundation of growth.

The Truth

It Takes 3–6 Months for Traction, Minimum

And 1–3 years to see a real, sustained difference. Every founder who burns out at month two loses the compounding effect that kicks in at month four. SMAC keeps you in it long enough to win — plus the option to continue.

⚡

The villain isn't laziness. It's isolation. When you're a solo founder with no team, no deadlines, and no one watching — consistency is the first thing to go. SMAC fixes the environment, not the willpower. And right now, it's the only active program of its kind built specifically for solopreneurs.

The Plan

Here's How It Works

Three steps. No overwhelm. Just show up.

01

Register for Round 3

One payment. Three months of accountability, community, coaching, and cash prize eligibility. Secure your spot before Round 3 closes.

02

Hit Your Weekly Minimum

Posting requirements ramp up each month — starting easy and building your habit progressively. Miss a week? Grace periods keep you in the game.

03

Grow & Win

All-Stars who hit requirements every week are entered to win. Finishers who make it to the end are entered too. Two ways to win, so just keep showing up. Consistency is rewarded — literally.

Why SMAC Works

You Don't Need More Tips. You Need Someone in Your Corner.

SMAC was built by Nate Templeton — a fellow solopreneur and full-time videographer who got tired of watching talented founders disappear into silence, but was also searching for accountability for himself.

Round 1 ran FREE from December 2025 through February 2026 and brought together 60+ founders. They showed up. They posted. They proved that structure is the missing piece — not motivation.

SMAC lives inside an active Instagram Group Chat where members post wins, cheer each other on, and hold each other accountable in real time. When your community engages with your content, the algorithm notices — and so does your audience. More engagement means more reach. More reach means more opportunities.

Nate will be in the trenches with you every step of the way.

Round 3 Roadmap

Your 90-Day Plan

Requirements ramp up each month so you build the habit before you build the volume.

Month 1 · March

Build the Habit

Ease back in. Grace period until March 14. Get oriented, reconnect with the community, and find your rhythm. One post a week is the bar — clear it every time.

Min: 1 post / week
Month 2 · April

Pick Up the Pace

Double your output. Start learning what your audience responds to and refine your content mix. This is where your strategy starts to take shape.

Min: 2 posts / week
Month 3 · May

Full Send

This is where compound consistency kicks in. Three months of showing up starts to look like real audience growth. Finish strong — All-Star status is on the line.

Min: 3 posts / week

What If You Do It For Free By Yourself?

You might make it happen on your own — but there's no group accountability, no community cheering you on, no structure keeping you honest, and no education to make sure you're posting the right things. You'll watch a YouTube video or jump into a free challenge, feel motivated for a week, and then hit a wall with nobody to pull you back. Or it's another 90 days of "I'll post more next week" and nothing happens. Another quarter of watching your competitors show up while you stay invisible. The algorithm rewards consistency and punishes absence. $45 is not the risk. Another quarter of silence is.

Common Questions

Still on the Fence?

Every objection, answered honestly.

Why do I need SMAC? Can't I just post on my own?
You could. And you've probably tried. The problem isn't knowing you should post — it's actually doing it when no one is watching and nothing feels urgent. SMAC exists because consistency is a social problem, not a knowledge problem. When you're in a room with 60+ other solopreneurs who are all showing up, sharing wins, calling each other out, and cheering each other on, "I'll post next week" stops being an option. The community doesn't just hold you accountable — it reminds you why you're doing this in the first place. Going at it alone means your motivation lives or dies by your mood. Going at it with SMAC means you have 60 other people who won't let you disappear.
I don't have time to post consistently.
That's exactly why the requirements ramp up gradually. Month 1 is one post a week — less than 30 minutes. The challenge builds your habit before it builds your volume. If you can't find 30 minutes a week, this isn't the right time. But it probably is, and you know it.
I'm not sure this will work for my industry or niche.
Round 1 had 60+ founders across different niches, service types, and industries. The principles of consistency and accountability are universal. Social media growth takes 3–6 months for traction — that's true regardless of niche. SMAC gives you the structure to stay in the game long enough for it to pay off.
I've tried challenges before and they never stick.
Most challenges fail because the accountability is soft and the community is passive. SMAC tracks your posting requirements, has real stakes (cash prizes), and is run by someone in the trenches with you — not a faceless course platform. The 60 founders who finished Round 1 had that same doubt.
Is $45 really worth it? What's the ROI?
One client, one sale, or one lead from your consistent content pays back $45 a hundred times over. The giveaway at the end of the round is a bonus. The real ROI is 90 days of showing up in front of your audience while your competitors stay quiet. That compounds.
What if I don't meet the posting requirements?
Miss the weekly minimum two weeks in a row and you'll be removed from the Instagram Group Chat — that's the accountability part. But it's not the end. If you're willing to recommit to the requirements, you're welcome back anytime, no questions asked. And no matter what happens with the IG group, you keep full access to the community platform for the duration of your round. You paid for it, it's yours.

Your Audience
Is Waiting.
